# awscc_s3files_file_system (Resource)

Resource Type definition for AWS::S3Files::FileSystem



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bucket` (String)
- `role_arn` (String)

### Optional

- `accept_bucket_warning` (Boolean)
- `client_token` (String)
- `kms_key_id` (String)
- `prefix` (String)
- `synchronization_configuration` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--synchronization_configuration))
- `tags` (Attributes Set) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `creation_time` (String)
- `file_system_arn` (String)
- `file_system_id` (String)
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `owner_id` (String)
- `status` (String)
- `status_message` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_s3files_file_system.example
  identity = {
    file_system_arn = "file_system_arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `file_system_arn` (String)

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_s3files_file_system.example
  id = "file_system_arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_s3files_file_system.example "file_system_arn"
```
